---
title: "Additional Information"
subtitle: "Methods Reference and Extended Learning Resources"
description: "Reference table for IPD methods used in the workshop plus additional resources for continued learning."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
---

## Methods Reference Table

| Method | `ipd` argument | Typical inferential model(s) | Core idea | Primary reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Chen and Chen | `"chen"` | OLS, logistic, poisson, mean/quantile settings | Anchors inference on labeled data and augments with proxy-based correction terms. | [Gronsbell et al. (2024)](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.19908) |
| PostPI (analytic) | `"postpi_analytic"` | OLS / GLM-style downstream tasks | Uses an analytic correction to post-prediction inference bias/variance. | [Wang et al. (2020)](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2001238117) |
| PostPI (bootstrap) | `"postpi_boot"` | OLS / GLM-style downstream tasks | Uses bootstrap calibration for uncertainty under predicted outcomes. | [Wang et al. (2020)](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2001238117) |
| PPI | `"ppi"` | OLS, logistic, poisson, mean/quantile | Prediction-powered augmentation balancing labeled and unlabeled information. | [Angelopoulos et al. (2023)](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adi6000) |
| PPI++ | `"ppi_plusplus"` | OLS, logistic, poisson, mean/quantile | Data-adaptive weighting extension of PPI for stronger finite-sample behavior. | [Angelopoulos et al. (2024)](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.01453) |
| PPI using all data | `"ppi_a"` | OLS, logistic, poisson, mean/quantile | Variant leveraging all available data with prediction-informed correction. | [Gronsbell et al. (2024)](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.19908) |
| PSPA | `"pspa"` | OLS, logistic, poisson, mean/quantile | Assumption-lean adaptive weighting of naive vs classical components. | [Miao et al. (2023)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.14220) |

::: {.callout-note}
## Practical Interpretation

Across methods, the common goal is the same: use predicted outcomes to gain efficiency while preserving valid statistical inference (unbiased effects and calibrated uncertainty).
:::

## Foundational and Package References

- `ipd` package paper: [Salerno et al. (2025), Bioinformatics](https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article/41/2/btaf055/7997267)
- `ipd` GitHub repository: [https://github.com/ipd-tools/ipd](https://github.com/ipd-tools/ipd)
- General IPD framework overview: [Ji et al. (2025)](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.09731)

## Additional Useful Resources for Learners

### Reproducible Analysis Workflow

- `tidyverse` reference: [https://www.tidyverse.org/](https://www.tidyverse.org/)
- `broom` tidying model outputs: [https://broom.tidymodels.org/](https://broom.tidymodels.org/)
- `patchwork` for multi-panel plots: [https://patchwork.data-imaginist.com/](https://patchwork.data-imaginist.com/)

### Model Diagnostics and Evaluation

- `pROC` for ROC/AUC analysis: [https://xrobin.github.io/pROC/](https://xrobin.github.io/pROC/)
- `DALEX` for model explainability: [https://modeloriented.github.io/DALEX/](https://modeloriented.github.io/DALEX/)

### Data Sources Used in the Workshop

- NHANES: [https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/index.html](https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/index.html)
- AlphaFold publication context: [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-03819-2](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-021-03819-2)

## Suggested Post-Workshop Practice

1. Re-run Unit 00 with different labeled/unlabeled ratios and compare method sensitivity.
2. In Unit 01 or Unit 02, replace one predictor set and re-evaluate naive vs IPD shifts.
3. Benchmark at least two IPD methods (`pspa` vs `ppi_plusplus`) on the same task and compare CI behavior.
